mission.title: kagent: expose kagent functionality through the API for external consumption
mission.description: expose kagent functionality through the API for external consumption. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kagent deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kagent version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent
helm list -n kagent 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kagent installation.

steps.title: Review kagent configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kagent configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent
kubectl get configmap -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kagent
```
Feedback/request from the discord: https://discord.com/channels/1346225185166065826/1346225185841221644/1351530953939484705

steps.title: Apply the fix for expose kagent functionality through the API for external…
steps.description:
This commit adds detailed logging to all REST API handlers in the controller:

- Add structured logging with context information (operation type, user ID, resource IDs)
- Implement different log levels (Info for important events, V(1) for detailed operations)
- Enhance error logging with detailed context
- Improve validation error messages in the team creation handler
- Create consistent logging patterns across all handlers
- Add logging to helper functions for complete traceability

The

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent
kubectl get events -n kagent --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "expose kagent functionality through the API for external…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This commit adds detailed logging to all REST API handlers in the controller:

- Add structured logging with context information (operation type, user ID, resource IDs)
- Implement different log levels (Info for important events, V(1) for detailed operations)
- Enhance error logging with detailed context
- Improve validation error messages in the team creation handler
- Create consistent logging

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kagent installed or the issue environment reproducible.
